evidence_excerpt: Possible treatment with bone marrow transplantation was not undertaken, and unfortunately, the patient passed away not long after due to septicaemia.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Despite prolonged intravenous antibiotic therapy, the abscesses failed to resolve completely, with multiple abscesses (including brain) and patchy lung consolidation.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Despite prophylactic measures, she continued to develop recurrent pyogenic infections.
Some infections required admission to hospital for intravenous antibacterial therapy and drainage of abscesses.
Drainage of abscesses was performed during hospital admissions.
CT showed three enhancing nodules in the right occipital lobe consistent with small abscesses and multiple soft-tissue abscesses; lung imaging showed patchy consolidation.
Bone marrow aspiration revealed features suggestive of myelodysplastic syndrome progressing to acute myeloid leukemia.
The patient passed away not long after due to septicaemia.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates for events after diagnosis are not provided; events span years after infancy. Time periods are categorized as long_term since they occurred well beyond 12 months after the diagnosis at 4 months of age. Functional status is inferred from recurrent admissions and persistent severe infections; specific ADL details are not described.
